title: banksia lepidorhiza
intro: Banksia lepidorhiza, also known as the hairy-leaved banksia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian medicine, with its leaves and seeds exhibiting medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The seeds of Banksia lepidorhiza are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which provide a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, with the leaves and seeds used to make a medicinal tonic.
